result.stdout:
SnmpSender:
  Sends snmp messages

Usage:
  SnmpSender [options] [command]

Options:
  --version    Display version information

Commands:
  get <objectId>       
  set                  
  trap <enterprise>

commands.payload:
SnmpSender:
  Sends snmp messages

Usage:
  SnmpSender [options] [command]

Options:
  --version    Display version information

Commands:
  get <objectId>       
  set                  
  trap <enterprise>

result.stdout:
Usage: SnmpSender get [options] Arguments: The ObjectId to send Options: -p, --port

Port to use for messages(default 161) -c, --community Community of message(default public) -v, --version Snmp version of the message(default 2) -a, --address Ipaddress to send message to(default 127.0.0.1)

commands.payload:
Usage: SnmpSender get [options] Arguments: The ObjectId to send Options: -p, --port

Port to use for messages(default 161) -c, --community Community of message(default public) -v, --version Snmp version of the message(default 2) -a, --address Ipaddress to send message to(default 127.0.0.1)

result.stdout:
Usage: SnmpSender set [options] Options: -p, --port

Port to use for messages(default 161) -c, --community Community of message(default public) -v, --version Snmp version of the message(default 2) -a, --address Ipaddress to send message to(default 127.0.0.1) -s, --setVar Variables to set. Use format objectId,type,value. Where type can be s(string),i(int),u(uint)

commands.payload:
Usage: SnmpSender set [options] Options: -p, --port

Port to use for messages(default 161) -c, --community Community of message(default public) -v, --version Snmp version of the message(default 2) -a, --address Ipaddress to send message to(default 127.0.0.1) -s, --setVar Variables to set. Use format objectId,type,value. Where type can be s(string),i(int),u(uint)

result.stdout:
Usage: SnmpSender trap [options] Arguments: The Enterprise of trap Options: -p, --port

Port to use for messages(default 161) -c, --community Community of message(default public) -v, --version Snmp version of the message(default 2) -a, --address Ipaddress to send message to(default 127.0.0.1) -s, --setVar Variables to set. Use format objectId,type,value. Where type can be s(string),i(int),u(uint) -r, --requestId Optional requestId of message

commands.payload:
Usage: SnmpSender trap [options] Arguments: The Enterprise of trap Options: -p, --port

Port to use for messages(default 161) -c, --community Community of message(default public) -v, --version Snmp version of the message(default 2) -a, --address Ipaddress to send message to(default 127.0.0.1) -s, --setVar Variables to set. Use format objectId,type,value. Where type can be s(string),i(int),u(uint) -r, --requestId Optional requestId of message
